fields.name: HSIKERDIV
fields.description: HSI kernel clock division factor This bitfield controlled by software sets the division factor of the kernel clock divider to produce HSIKER clock:

fields.name: HSION
fields.description: HSI clock enable Set and cleared by software and hardware, with hardware taking priority. Kept low by hardware as long as the device is in a low-power mode. Kept high by hardware as long as the system is clocked with a clock derived from HSI. This includes the exit from low-power modes and the system clock fall-back to HSI upon failing HSE oscillator clock selected as system clock source.

fields.name: HSIKERON
fields.description: HSI always-enable for peripheral kernels. Set and cleared by software. Setting the bit activates the HSI oscillator in Run and Stop modes, regardless of the HSION bit state. The HSI clock can only feed USART1, USART2, and I2C1 peripherals configured with HSI as kernel clock. Note: Keeping the HSI active in Stop mode allows speeding up the serial interface communication as the HSI clock is ready immediately upon exiting Stop mode.

fields.name: HSIRDY
fields.description: HSI clock ready flag Set by hardware when the HSI oscillator is enabled through HSION and ready to use (stable). Note: Upon clearing HSION, HSIRDY goes low after six HSI clock cycles.

fields.name: HSIDIV
fields.description: HSI clock division factor This bitfield controlled by software sets the division factor of the HSI clock divider to produce HSISYS clock:

fields.name: HSEON
fields.description: HSE clock enable Set and cleared by software. Cleared by hardware to stop the HSE oscillator when entering Stop, or Standby, or Shutdown mode. This bit cannot be cleared if the HSE oscillator is used directly or indirectly as the system clock.

fields.name: HSERDY
fields.description: HSE clock ready flag Set by hardware to indicate that the HSE oscillator is stable and ready for use. Note: Upon clearing HSEON, HSERDY goes low after six HSE clock cycles.

fields.name: HSEBYP
fields.description: HSE crystal oscillator bypass Set and cleared by software. When the bit is set, the internal HSE oscillator is bypassed for use of an external clock. The external clock must then be enabled with the HSEON bit set. Write access to the bit is only effective when the HSE oscillator is disabled.

fields.name: CSSON
fields.description: Clock security system enable Set by software to enable the clock security system. When the bit is set, the clock detector is enabled by hardware when the HSE oscillator is ready, and disabled by hardware if a HSE clock failure is detected. The bit is cleared by hardware upon reset.

registers.name: ICSCR
registers.description: RCC internal clock source calibration register
registers.fieldset_description: RCC internal clock source calibration register

fields.name: HSICAL
fields.description: HSI clock calibration This bitfield directly acts on the HSI clock frequency. Its value is a sum of an internal factory-programmed number and the value of the HSITRIM[6:0] bitfield. In the factory, the internal number is set to calibrate the HSI clock frequency to 48 MHz (with HSITRIM[6:0] left at its reset value). Refer to the device datasheet for HSI calibration accuracy and for the frequency trimming granularity. Note: The trimming effect presents discontinuities at HSICAL[7:0] multiples of 64.

fields.name: HSITRIM
fields.description: HSI clock trimming The value of this bitfield contributes to the HSICAL[7:0] bitfield value. It allows HSI clock frequency user trimming. The HSI frequency accuracy as stated in the device datasheet applies when this bitfield is left at its reset value.

fields.name: SW
fields.description: System clock switch This bitfield is controlled by software and hardware. The bitfield selects the clock for SYSCLK as follows: Others: Reserved The setting is forced by hardware to 000 (HSISYS selected) when the MCU exits Stop, or Standby, or Shutdown mode, or when the setting is 001 (HSE selected) and HSE oscillator failure is detected.

fields.name: SWS
fields.description: System clock switch status This bitfield is controlled by hardware to indicate the clock source used as system clock: Others: Reserved

fields.name: HPRE
fields.description: AHB prescaler This bitfield is controlled by software. To produce HCLK clock, it sets the division factor of SYSCLK clock as follows: 0xxx: 1

fields.name: PPRE
fields.description: APB prescaler This bitfield is controlled by software. To produce PCLK clock, it sets the division factor of HCLK clock as follows: 0xx: 1

fields.name: MCO2SEL
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output 2 clock selector This bitfield is controlled by software. It sets the clock selector for MCO2 output as follows: This bitfield is controlled by software. It sets the clock selector for MCO output as follows: Note: This clock output may have some truncated cycles at startup or during MCO2 clock source switching.

fields.name: MCO2PRE
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output 2 prescaler This bitfield is controlled by software. It sets the division factor of the clock sent to the MCO2 output as follows: ... It is highly recommended to set this field before the MCO2 output is enabled.

fields.name: MCO1SEL
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output clock selector This bitfield is controlled by software. It sets the clock selector for MCO output as follows: Note: This clock output may have some truncated cycles at startup or during MCO clock source switching. Any other value means no clock on MCO.

fields.name: MCO1PRE
fields.description: Microcontroller clock output prescaler This bitfield is controlled by software. It sets the division factor of the clock sent to the MCO output as follows: ... It is highly recommended to set this field before the MCO output is enabled.

fields.name: LSIRDYIE
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the LSI oscillator stabilization:

fields.name: LSERDYIE
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the LSE oscillator stabilization:

fields.name: HSIRDYIE
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the HSI oscillator stabilization:

fields.name: HSERDYIE
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable interrupt caused by the HSE oscillator stabilization:

fields.name: LSIRDYF
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon LSE clock getting ready. Set by hardware when the LSI clock becomes stable and LSIRDYDIE is set. Cleared by software setting the LSIRDYC bit.

fields.name: LSERDYF
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon LSE clock getting ready. Set by hardware when the LSE clock becomes stable and LSERDYDIE is set. Cleared by software setting the LSERDYC bit.

fields.name: HSIRDYF
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon HSI clock getting ready. Set by hardware when the HSI clock becomes stable and HSIRDYIE is set in response to setting the HSION (refer to ). When HSION is not set but the HSI oscillator is enabled by the peripheral through a clock request, this bit is not set and no interrupt is generated. Cleared by software setting the HSIRDYC bit.

fields.name: HSERDYF
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon HSE clock getting ready. Set by hardware when the HSE clock becomes stable and HSERDYIE is set. Cleared by software setting the HSERDYC bit.

fields.name: CSSF
fields.description: HSE clock security system interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon HSE clock failure. Set by hardware when a failure is detected in the HSE oscillator. Cleared by software setting the CSSC bit.

fields.name: LSECSSF
fields.description: LSE clock security system interrupt flag This flag indicates a pending interrupt upon LSE clock failure. Set by hardware when a failure is detected in the LSE oscillator. Cleared by software by setting the LSECSSC bit.

fields.name: LSIRDYC
fields.description: LSI ready interrupt clear This bit is set by software to clear the LSIRDYF flag.

fields.name: LSERDYC
fields.description: LSE ready interrupt clear This bit is set by software to clear the LSERDYF flag.

fields.name: HSIRDYC
fields.description: HSI ready interrupt clear This bit is set software to clear the HSIRDYF flag.

fields.name: HSERDYC
fields.description: HSE ready interrupt clear This bit is set by software to clear the HSERDYF flag.

fields.name: CSSC
fields.description: Clock security system interrupt clear This bit is set by software to clear the HSECSSF flag.

fields.name: LSECSSC
fields.description: LSE Clock security system interrupt clear This bit is set by software to clear the LSECSSF flag.

fields.name: GPIOARST
fields.description: I/O port A reset This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOBRST
fields.description: I/O port B reset This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOCRST
fields.description: I/O port C reset This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIODRST
fields.description: I/O port D reset This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOFRST
fields.description: I/O port F reset This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: DMA1RST
fields.description: DMA1 and DMAMUX reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: FLASHRST
fields.description: Flash memory interface reset Set and cleared by software. This bit can only be set when the Flash memory is in power down mode.

fields.name: CRCRST
fields.description: CRC reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM3RST
fields.description: TIM3 timer reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: USART2RST
fields.description: USART2 reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: I2C1RST
fields.description: I2C1 reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: DBGRST
fields.description: Debug support reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: PWRRST
fields.description: Power interface reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: SYSCFGRST
fields.description: SYSCFG reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM1RST
fields.description: TIM1 timer reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: SPI1RST
fields.description: SPI1 reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: USART1RST
fields.description: USART1 reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM14RST
fields.description: TIM14 timer reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM16RST
fields.description: TIM16 timer reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM17RST
fields.description: TIM16 timer reset Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: ADC1RST
fields.description: ADC reset Set and cleared by software.

registers.description: RCC I/O port clock enable register
registers.fieldset_description: RCC I/O port clock enable register

fields.name: GPIOAEN
fields.description: I/O port A clock enable This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOBEN
fields.description: I/O port B clock enable This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOCEN
fields.description: I/O port C clock enable This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIODEN
fields.description: I/O port D clock enable This bit is set and cleared by software.

fields.name: GPIOFEN
fields.description: I/O port F clock enable This bit is set and cleared by software.

registers.name: AHBENR
registers.description: RCC AHB peripheral clock enable register
registers.fieldset_description: RCC AHB peripheral clock enable register

fields.name: DMA1EN
fields.description: DMA1 and DMAMUX clock enable Set and cleared by software. DMAMUX is enabled as long as at least one DMA peripheral is enabled.

fields.name: FLASHEN
fields.description: Flash memory interface clock enable Set and cleared by software. This bit can only be cleared when the Flash memory is in power down mode.

fields.name: CRCEN
fields.description: CRC clock enable Set and cleared by software.

registers.name: APBENR1
registers.description: RCC APB peripheral clock enable register 1
registers.fieldset_description: RCC APB peripheral clock enable register 1

fields.name: TIM3EN
fields.description: TIM3 timer clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: RTCAPBEN
fields.description: RTC APB clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: WWDGEN
fields.description: WWDG clock enable Set by software to enable the window watchdog clock. Cleared by hardware system reset This bit can also be set by hardware if the WWDG_SW option bit is 0.

fields.name: USART2EN
fields.description: USART2 clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: I2C1EN
fields.description: I2C1 clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: DBGEN
fields.description: Debug support clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: PWREN
fields.description: Power interface clock enable Set and cleared by software.

registers.name: APBENR2
registers.description: RCC APB peripheral clock enable register 2
registers.fieldset_description: RCC APB peripheral clock enable register 2

fields.name: SYSCFGEN
fields.description: SYSCFG clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM1EN
fields.description: TIM1 timer clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: SPI1EN
fields.description: SPI1 clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: USART1EN
fields.description: USART1 clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM14EN
fields.description: TIM14 timer clock enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: TIM16EN

fields.name: ADC1EN

fields.name: GPIOASMEN

fields.name: GPIOBSMEN

registers.name: AHBSMENR

fields.name: DMA1SMEN
fields.description: DMA1 and DMAMUX clock enable during Sleep mode Set and cleared by software. Clock to DMAMUX during Sleep mode is enabled as long as the clock in Sleep mode is enabled to at least one DMA peripheral.

fields.name: FLASHSMEN
fields.description: Flash memory interface clock enable during Sleep mode Set and cleared by software. This bit can be activated only when the Flash memory is in power down mode.

fields.name: SRAMSMEN

fields.name: CRCSMEN

fields.name: SPI1SMEN

registers.name: CCIPR
registers.description: RCC peripherals independent clock configuration register
registers.fieldset_description: RCC peripherals independent clock configuration register

fields.name: USART1SEL
fields.description: USART1 clock source selection This bitfield is controlled by software to select USART1 clock source as follows:

fields.name: I2C1SEL
fields.description: I2C1 clock source selection This bitfield is controlled by software to select I2C1 clock source as follows:

fields.name: I2S1SEL
fields.description: I2S1 clock source selection This bitfield is controlled by software to select I2S1 clock source as follows:

fields.name: ADC1SEL
fields.description: ADCs clock source selection This bitfield is controlled by software to select the clock source for ADC:

fields.name: LSEON
fields.description: LSE oscillator enable Set and cleared by software to enable LSE oscillator:

fields.name: LSERDY
fields.description: LSE oscillator ready Set and cleared by hardware to indicate when the external 32 kHz oscillator is ready (stable): After the LSEON bit is cleared, LSERDY goes low after 6 external low-speed oscillator clock cycles.

fields.name: LSEBYP
fields.description: LSE oscillator bypass Set and cleared by software to bypass the LSE oscillator (in debug mode). This bit can be written only when the external 32 kHz oscillator is disabled (LSEON=0 and LSERDY=0).

fields.name: LSEDRV
fields.description: LSE oscillator drive capability Set by software to select the LSE oscillator drive capability as follows: Applicable when the LSE oscillator is in Xtal mode, as opposed to bypass mode.

fields.name: LSECSSON
fields.description: CSS on LSE enable Set by software to enable the clock security system on LSE (32 kHz) oscillator as follows: LSECSSON must be enabled after the LSE oscillator is enabled (LSEON bit enabled) and ready (LSERDY flag set by hardware), and after the RTCSEL bit is selected. Once enabled, this bit cannot be disabled, except after a LSE failure detection (LSECSSD =1). In that case the software must disable the LSECSSON bit.

fields.name: LSECSSD
fields.description: CSS on LSE failure Detection Set by hardware to indicate when a failure is detected by the clock security system on the external 32 kHz oscillator (LSE):

fields.name: RTCSEL
fields.description: RTC clock source selection Set by software to select the clock source for the RTC as follows: Once the RTC clock source is selected, it cannot be changed anymore unless the RTC domain is reset, or unless a failure is detected on LSE (LSECSSD is set). The RTCRST bit can be used to reset this bitfield to 00.

fields.name: RTCEN
fields.description: RTC clock enable Set and cleared by software. The bit enables clock to RTC and TAMP.

fields.name: RTCRST
fields.description: RTC domain software reset Set and cleared by software to reset the RTC domain:

fields.name: LSCOEN
fields.description: Low-speed clock output (LSCO) enable Set and cleared by software.

fields.name: LSCOSEL
fields.description: Low-speed clock output selection Set and cleared by software to select the low-speed output clock:

fields.name: LSION
fields.description: LSI oscillator enable Set and cleared by software to enable/disable the LSI oscillator:

fields.name: LSIRDY
fields.description: LSI oscillator ready Set and cleared by hardware to indicate when the LSI oscillator is ready (stable): After the LSION bit is cleared, LSIRDY goes low after 3 LSI oscillator clock cycles. This bit can be set even if LSION = 0 if the LSI is requested by the Clock Security System on LSE, by the Independent Watchdog or by the RTC.

fields.name: RMVF
fields.description: Remove reset flags Set by software to clear the reset flags.

fields.name: OBLRSTF
fields.description: Option byte loader reset flag Set by hardware when a reset from the Option byte loading occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: PINRSTF
fields.description: Pin reset flag Set by hardware when a reset from the NRST pin occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: PWRRSTF
fields.description: BOR or POR/PDR flag Set by hardware when a BOR or POR/PDR occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: SFTRSTF
fields.description: Software reset flag Set by hardware when a software reset occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: IWDGRSTF
fields.description: Independent window watchdog reset flag Set by hardware when an independent watchdog reset domain occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: WWDGRSTF
fields.description: Window watchdog reset flag Set by hardware when a window watchdog reset occurs. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit.

fields.name: LPWRRSTF
fields.description: Low-power reset flag Set by hardware when a reset occurs due to illegal Stop, or Standby, or Shutdown mode entry. Cleared by setting the RMVF bit. This operates only if nRST_STOP, or nRST_STDBY or nRST_SHDW option bits are cleared.

variants.name: Div16
variants.description: MCO clock is divided divided by 16

variants.name: Div32
variants.description: MCO clock is divided divided by 32

variants.name: Div64
variants.description: MCO clock is divided divided by 64

variants.name: Div128
variants.description: MCO clock is divided divided by 128

variants.name: DISABLE
variants.description: No clock used as RTC clock

variants.name: HSE_DIV_32
variants.description: HSE divided by 32 used as RTC clock

variants.name: HSISYS
variants.description: HSISYS (HSI divided by HSIDIV) selected as system clock
